isolation.workspaceOwnership: Single validator used the repo-root Gradle workspace and shared build outputs for this milestone.

assertions.title: Wrapper-first bootstrap exposes the expected subprojects
assertions.reason: The checked-in wrapper files were present and `./gradlew projects` succeeded from repo root while listing `:core` and `:test-suite`.

evidence.command: python3 -c "from pathlib import Path; import json; root=Path('.'); print(json.dumps({'gradlew': (root/'gradlew').is_file(), 'wrapperJar': (root/'gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.jar').is_file(), 'wrapperProperties': (root/'gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.properties').is_file()}))"
evidence.observation: Confirmed the checked-in wrapper script, wrapper JAR, and wrapper properties file all exist at the repository root.
Gradle project discovery succeeded from the wrapper entrypoint and reported root project `e2e` with subprojects `:core` and `:test-suite`.

assertions.title: Root build targets Java 17 and validates both modules together
assertions.reason: The root `check` path completed successfully with Java 17 and scheduled both `:core` and `:test-suite` tasks from one repo-root command.

evidence.observation: The build ran from repo root, selected the root `check` task, and executed the cross-module task graph including `:core:check`/`:core:test` plus `:test-suite:check`/`:test-suite:test`.
Gradle reported an available Azul Zulu JDK with `Language Version: 17`, matching the Java 17 toolchain requirement used by the build.

assertions.title: Root task discovery exposes concrete execution and reporting entrypoints
assertions.reason: Root task discovery showed both a sample execution entrypoint and Allure reporting entrypoints without opening build scripts.

evidence.observation: Task output included `sampleTest`, `test-suite:test`, `allureReport`, and `allureServe`, giving concrete execution and reporting entrypoints from the root CLI.

assertions.title: Reporting prerequisites fail clearly before test execution
assertions.reason: After clearing generated build outputs, `allureReport` returned a readable, actionable outcome instead of an opaque failure when no test results existed.

evidence.observation: The task was skipped cleanly and printed `No Allure results found ... Run ./gradlew sampleTest --no-daemon or ./gradlew :test-suite:test --no-daemon before generating the report.`

assertions.title: Both submodules are directly addressable from the repo root
assertions.reason: A single repo-root command addressed both submodules directly and returned task listings for `:core` and `:test-suite`.

evidence.observation: Gradle printed task listings for project `:test-suite` and project `:core`, confirming both subprojects are directly targetable from repo root.

summary: Tested 5 CLI/Gradle assertions for bootstrap-reporting: 5 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked.
